key_insight: GeoAlign stabilizes online LLM reinforcement learning by detecting and replacing rollouts whose reward-induced update directions conflict with the batch consensus.
engineering_relevance: It is a lightweight forward-pass-only curation module that can improve robustness of iterative policy optimization under noisy or misspecified rewards.
method: Forms within-prompt preference pairs from rollouts.
Learns an online projector on hidden states to concentrate reward-ordered displacement directions.
Flags directionally inconsistent rollouts by angular deviation from a batch prototype and swaps them with stable alternatives.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports improved final performance and reduced training oscillation on dialogue alignment with a learned reward model and mathematical reasoning with binary verified rewards, outperforming PF-PPO, PAR, PODS, and Seed-GRPO.
watch_item: The approach depends on reliable within-prompt alternatives and a meaningful batch consensus, which may be harder to estimate when rewards are extremely sparse or batch composition is poor.
paper_title: GEOALIGN: Geometric Rollout Curation for Robust LLM Reinforcement Learning
